rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. PROPERTY SUBJECT TO DISPOSITION: $2,279.31 U.S. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order That Requests For Admissions Set No. One Be Deemed Admitted; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declarations Of Victoria Robinson And Maite Kvarna In Support Thereof; Exhibits; [Proposed Order]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion & Discovery Calendar for Monday. March 16, 2026, Line 1, PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA's Motion For Order That Requests For Admissions Set No. One Be Deemed Admitted. Petitioner People of the State of California's motion for order deeming set one requests for admissions admitted. Granted. Good cause shown and no opposition filed. All requests in the People's set one requests for admission are deemed admitted. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JUNHAI BAI ET AL VS. STEPHANIE YIP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Timely Amend
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion & Discovery Calendar for Monday. March 16, 2026, Line 8, DEFENDANTS STEPHANIE YIP and SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT's Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Timely Amend. Defendants Stephanie Yip and the San Francisco Unified School District's motion to dismiss for failure to timely amend is granted. This case is now dismissed in its entirety with prejudice. On September 17, 2025 the court sustained defendants' demurrer with thirty days leave to amend to the document filed by plaintiffs Junhai Bai and Xiaofei Li which was referred to and deemed to be their first amended complaint. Despite being informed by the court on multiple occasions, including January 14, 2026 and March 4, 2026, that their failure to file a second amended complaint would result in dismissal of this case, plaintiffs have chosen not to file a second amended complaint. This court has also repeatedly told plaintiffs that their position that they do not have to file a second amended complaint to proceed with their case due to a ruling by the Court of Appeal lacks merit. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD K. EINSELEN VS. ARENA NIGHTLIFE GROUP, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion & Discovery Calendar for Monday. March 16, 2026, Line 2, DEFENDANT ARENA NIGHTLIFE GROUP, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Arena Nightlife Group, LLC's demurrer to all three causes of action alleged in the second amended complaint is overruled as to the first cause of action for breach of contract and off calendar as to the second and third causes of action for unfair competition and financial elder abuse. Plaintiff Richard Einselen adequately alleges a claim that he was damaged by ANG's failure to pay the loan it assumed in the stock purchase agreement and that the failure to pay was a breach of the stock purchase agreement. rulings.Case Title: INFLECT, INC. ET AL VS. SOLANA FOUNDATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion & Discovery Calendar for Monday. March 16, 2026, Line 10, DEFENDANT SOLANA FOUNDATION, A SWISS FOUNDATION'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. (Part 1 of 2 for purposes of entry of Tentative Ruling.) Defendant Solana Foundation's demurrer to all seven causes of action alleged by plaintiff Inflect, Inc. in the first amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to the third cause of action for quantum meruit and overruled as to all other causes of action. Solana's unopposed request for judicial notice is granted. Solana's late-filed reply memorandum was considered by the court. As to the first and second causes of action for breach of written contract and breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, Inflect, Inc. sufficiently alleged that it is a third-party beneficiary of the Server Inventory Services Reduction Agreement (Agreement). Determination of whether a person or entity is a third-party beneficiary is fact-intensive based on all the relevant circumstances of the making of the contract. Nothing in the Agreement rules out Inflect, Inc. as a third-party beneficiary. The fact that Inflect, Inc. was not to receive tokens does not preclude it from benefiting from the Agreement by receiving compensation other than in tokens and there is language in the Agreement supporting that Inflect, Inc.'s allegation that the named contracting parties intended that Inflect, Inc. would do so. As to the third cause of action for quantum meruit, the alleged basis for recovery (Inflect, Inc.'s performance of a cost-reduction services for Solana) is the same as the basis for recovery under the Agreement and thus runs afoul of the rule that quantum meruit recovery is not available where the same subject matter is addressed in an express contract. As to the fourth and fifth causes of action for intentional and negligent interference with prospective economic relations, Inflect, Inc. sufficiently alleged that Solana committed independently wrongful (tortious) conduct consisting of, among other things, actionable defamation and disparagement. Solana's arguments that all of the independent wrongful conduct allegedly committed by it are conclusions and/or non-actionable are belied by the language of the first amended complaint. As to the sixth and seventh causes of action for intentional interference with contractual relations and inducing breach of contract, Inflect, Inc. sufficiently alleged actionable interference by Solana with Inflect, Inc.'s contract with Service Provider 2. Contrary to Solana's reading of them, the judicially noticeable documents do not show that Inflect, Inc. committed an antecedent breach of its contract with Service Provider 2. (Tentative Ruling continues in Part 2 of 2.)

rulings.Case Title: JASPREET KALRA ET AL VS. THE REGENTS OF UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA; ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production To Defendant The Regents Of University Of California, Set One; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion & Discovery Calendar for Monday. March 16, 2026, Line 5, PLAINTIFFS JASPREET KALRA and NAVDEEP SINGH's Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production To Defendant The Regents Of University Of California, Set One; Request For Sanctions. Plaintiffs' motion to compel further response to their set one document requests from defendant The Regents of the University of California is continued to March 18, 2026 to give the parties one more opportunity to resolve the issues raised by this motion. The parties are required to meet and confer in person or by remote video platform no later than 5pm on March 16, 2026 in an effort to resolve all remaining issues raised by this motion. No later than 4pm on March 17 counsel for plaintiffs must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties succinctly stating, without argument, each remaining unresolved issue raised by thism motion. For guidance of the parties, the court believes that a party responding to document requests must identify, as to each request, whether and why any responsive documents have not and will not be produced and, if the reason for the non-production is an assertion of privilege, the following information about each withheld document other than documents to, from or written by outside litigation counsel after the case was filed must be provided: date of document, ground(s) of privilege, type of document (e.g. letter, memo), author(s) of document, all recipients of documents, and a brief description of the document. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Plaintiff Environmental Health Advocates, Inc.'s motion to compel further responses to set one document requests from defendant Bottom Line Food Processors and request for monetary sanctions is denied as moot as to the motion to compel and granted in part as to the request for monetary sanctions. No later than April 10, 2026 Bottom line and its counsel must pay $2,080 in reasonable monetary sanctions to Environmental Health. Bottom Line's recent service of supplemental responses moots the motion to compel. Bottom Line's long delay in revising the protective order causing Environmental Health to reasonably file this motion lacked substantial justification. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)
